## Save a forest plot to a file {.unnumbered}


### Description

Writes the plot produced by <code>forrest()</code> (or any base-R plotting code) to a
file. The graphics device is inferred from the file extension.



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>save_forrest(file, plot, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
</code></pre>


### Arguments

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="file">file</code></td>
<td>
Output file path. Supported extensions: <code>.pdf</code>, <code>.png</code>, <code>.svg</code>,
<code>.tiff</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="plot">plot</code></td>
<td>
A zero-argument function (or <code>function() { ... }</code> block) whose
body calls <code>forrest()</code>. It is evaluated inside the open graphics device.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="width">width</code></td>
<td>
Plot width in inches. Default <code>7</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="height">height</code></td>
<td>
Plot height in inches. Default <code>5</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="dpi">dpi</code></td>
<td>
Resolution in dots per inch for raster formats (<code>.png</code>, <code>.tiff</code>).
Ignored for vector formats (<code>.pdf</code>, <code>.svg</code>). Default <code>300</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="bg">bg</code></td>
<td>
Background colour. Default <code>"white"</code>.
</td></tr>
</table>


### Value

Invisibly returns <code>file</code>.



### Examples
```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, eval=TRUE}
library("forrest")

dat <- data.frame(
  label    = c("Exposure A", "Exposure B", "Exposure C"),
  estimate = c(0.42, -0.18, 0.31),
  lower    = c(0.22, -0.38, 0.12),
  upper    = c(0.62,  0.02, 0.50)
)
tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pdf")
save_forrest(tmp, function() {
  forrest(
    dat,
    estimate = "estimate",
    lower    = "lower",
    upper    = "upper",
    label    = "label",
    xlab     = "Regression coefficient (95% CI)"
  )
})



```
